label: Narrow-shell 1BR / 1 bath, LD style -- sibling of 1s_1br_nw_front_back_split_bath_gr for lots too big to stay compact: full-width living room across the front (hosts entry), dining and kitchen side-by-side in a mid band behind it, bedroom and bath side-by-side in the rear band (same private-band layout as the gr sibling).

adjacencies.note: living spans the full width; dining sits directly behind its left half -- open mouth into dining, no wall (ld style).

living's other open mouth, into kitchen behind its right half. Two separate open-plan seams along one continuous rear wall of the living room (same pattern as a hallway opening onto two rooms elsewhere in this catalog).
dining and kitchen sit side-by-side in the mid band -- open pass-through, no wall (PH practice, matches the working _ld precedents).

bedroom's only door -- opens into dining, its direct front neighbor in the same (left) column. Simpler than the gr sibling's kitchen-side door since dining is right there; no furniture-wall reason to route it further.
bedroom and bath share the rear band side-by-side -- shared wall only, no door, per the locked no-bath-through-bedroom rule.
bath (rear-right) stacks directly behind kitchen (mid-right) on one plumbing line -- shared wet wall AND the bath's only door, same wet-stack pattern as the gr sibling.

notes: Sibling of 1s_1br_nw_front_back_split_bath_gr, created 2026-07-20 for lots too
big to stay compact -- the gr sibling is now deliberately restricted to its
canonical 8x10 (see its own sweep fixture notes). This one trades the gr
sibling's counter-divider great_room for a real 3-band public sequence:
Front (full width): LIVING ROOM (entry)
Mid:   [DINING | KITCHEN]
Rear:  [BEDROOM | BATH ]
living is double-anchored (left AND right) so it always spans the full
envelope width, the same mechanism the front_rear_bath_gr sibling uses to
pin its full-width bedroom. Behind it the building reverts to the gr
sibling's familiar two-column structure: dining stacks where great used to
be (left column, above bedroom), kitchen and bath are UNCHANGED from the gr
sibling (right column, kitchen mid above bath rear, one wet-plumbing line).
bedroom's door now opens into dining (its direct front neighbor) rather
than reaching across to kitchen the way the gr sibling's does -- that
routing was specifically to keep the gr sibling's great_room wall clear
for furniture, which doesn't apply here since dining is a separate room.
kitchen_rear_pin: false and ldk_horizontal: true carried over from the gr
sibling for the same structural reasons (bath stacks behind kitchen, not
the rear wall; dining/kitchen are side-by-side, not vertically stacked).
No lot_adjustment_profiles yet -- untuned, pending real feasibility
testing at its intended med/max sizes (9x11, 10x12).
